title: I’ve fired one of America’s most powerful lasers—here’s what a shot day looks like
description: The laser was used to study the physics of stellar interiors and fusion energy, among other things.

title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

title: Great white sharks are overheating
description: The sharks might also be the most physiologically vulnerable to warming waters.

title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.

title: Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In
description: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.

title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
description: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

title: OpenAI Executive Kevin Weil Is Leaving the Company
description: The former Instagram VP is departing the ChatGPT-maker, which is folding the AI science application he led into Codex.

title: Man with @ihackedthegovernment Instagram account tells judge, “I made a mistake"
description: Probation for man who used stolen logins and posted private info on social media.

title: Trump picks qualified, normal health leader to head CDC; experts still cautious
description: She's well qualified but will need to navigate RFK Jr.'s anti-vaccine agenda.

title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder
description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?

title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the trade-off may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.

title: Pie Day 2026

description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
description: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto plants. title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
description: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
